steps.title: Set up the exponential growth model
steps.content: Exponential growth has the form $p(t) = p_0 \cdot r^t$, where $p_0$ is the initial population and $r$ is the annual growth factor. Here $p_0 = 24$ (the 1788 starting population) and $t$ is years after 1788.

steps.title: Convert 16.2% annual increase into a growth factor
steps.content: A 16.2% yearly increase means each year the population is multiplied by $1 + 0.162 = 1.162$. So $r = 1.162$.

steps.title: Write the model and check the choices
steps.content:
Substituting gives $p(t) = 24 \cdot 1.162^t$.
  • Choice A: $1.162 \cdot 24^t$ starts at 1.162 (not 24) and uses 24 as the base, which would explode far too quickly. Eliminated.
  • Choice B: $24 \cdot 2^{1.162t}$ starts at 24 but doubles roughly every year, an annual factor of $2^{1.162} \approx 2.24$, a 124% increase. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: $24 \cdot 1.162^t$ starts at 24 and grows by 16.2% each year. Matches.
  • Choice D: $(24 \cdot 1.162)^t = 27.888^t$ starts at 1 (when $t=0$), not 24. Eliminated.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
